title: Chouchou Refuses to Abandon the Shop
description: On pages 1–4, Chouchou’s loyalty to the pet food shop turns Buggy’s occupation from a pirate threat into a personal wound for the town.
scene: Chouchou refuses to give up the place connected to his owner, while pirates and bystanders react to the dog’s stubborn defense.

dialogue.speaker: Buggy
dialogue.text: BUT HOW? YOUR BODY DEMOLISHES A BUILDING AND YOU WALK AWAY WITHOUT A SCRATCH!!? THAT'S CRAZY!

dialogue.speaker: Mayor Boodle
dialogue.text: WHY DID YOU ALL COME TO THIS TOWN ANYWAY? WHY TAKE ON PIRATES?

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Just figured out why I'm here.

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: I'm after the map of the GRAND LINE... and a NAVIGATOR!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: Does he think he can stop us from entering that store?

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: DON'T TELL ME YOU'RE GUARDING THIS CRUMMY SHOP?

dialogue.speaker: Chouchou
dialogue.text: Look, chouchou! The store's all finished!

dialogue.speaker: Chouchou
dialogue.text: You and me will make it prosper together!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Having a rough time, eh? Fine, then I'll buy some pet food to eat!

dialogue.speaker: Chouchou
dialogue.text: Ha ha ha! You look like a dog, but I didn't know you ate like one, too!!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: You idiot! I'm trying to help you!

summary: This part makes the conflict emotional: Luffy sees that Buggy’s violence is not abstract, but aimed at things people love and protect.
